rule_clarifications: state precedence: peaked > heating > mature > emerging > undetermined
breadth requirement is min(2, n_sources) (single-source reality pre-keys)
peaked requires the expanding max composite to have reached L2 before now
A1 quantifies 'strongly negative accel'; 'high level' means composite >= L2
peak_proximity/drawdown computed on min-shifted composite (z can be negative)
rationale: Chosen by inspection among the four score-tied leaders (41.28/90) of the extended grid. L2=0.4 and V0=0.08 are the only grid values that keep Mature achievable at all on an expanding-z composite (structural plateau decay toward the expanding mean - see LABNOTES 2026-07-13); V1=0.08 is the middle velocity floor: the first Heating flag is identical to V1=0.05 (2022-08-07, inside the pre-registered window) with fewer marginal flags afterwards; A1=-0.12 keeps the 'strongly negative accel' Peaked branch strict, which also stops Peaked from cannibalising rare Mature weeks under the precedence rule. Disclosed shortfall, reported as-is: mature_share_2025_26 = 0.026 - the pre-registered 'mature by 2025' arc is NOT reproduced on real geo=IN data with the pre-registered rule forms; the real arc is rise 2022-23, peak 2024, oscillating decline to a still-elevated base.
